chapter_title.en: Chapter 33 – Cutting the hair and the shaving

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/379)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/379)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّ أَصْحَابَنَا يَرْوُونَ أَنَّ حَلْقَ الرَّأْسِ فِي غَيْرِ حَجٍّ وَ لَا عُمْرَةٍ مُثْلَةٌ فَقَالَ كَانَ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) إِذَا قَضَى مَنَاسِكَهُ عَدَلَ إِلَى قَرْيَةٍ يُقَالُ لَهَا سَايَةُ فَحَلَقَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr who said,

‘I said to Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> that our companions are reporting that shaving the head regarding other than Hajj or Umrah is a mutilation’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Whenever Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> used to fulfil his rituals, would go to a town called Sayah and shave (his<sup>asws</sup> head)’.
en.sarwar: 3. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr who has said the following: “I once said to abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘Our people narrate that shaving one’s head, except for al-Hajj or al-‘Umrah, is like causing deformity.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘Abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al- Salam, after al-Hajj and al-‘Umrah would go to the town called Sayah and shave his head.’”
